mission.title: ko: ko stops building containers after Golang updates
mission.description: ko stops building containers after Golang updates. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check ko installation
steps.description:
Verify your ko installation:
```bash
ko version
```
Install if needed: `go install github.com/google/ko@latest` or `brew install ko`. ko builds Go container images without Docker.

steps.title: Review the issue context
steps.description: After each minor Golang version bump (e.g. 1.13.2 -> 1.13.3), `ko apply` no longer builds container images but happily applies the yaml untransformed, which leads with image pull errors on the cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ko stops building containers after Golang updates
steps.description:
I think we've improved this situation by surfacing better errors and requiring `ko://`, but I'm not sure if the underlying problem is actually fixed. This reminds me of [a discussion on slack](https://knative.slack.com/archives/CAVRMBUMA/p1608591221217100) where the issue involved homebrew, and the
```yaml
$ go env
GO111MODULE=""
GOARCH="amd64"
GOBIN=""
GOCACHE="/Users/scothis/Library/Caches/go-build"
GOENV="/Users/scothis/Library/Application Support/go/env"
GOEXE=""
GOFLAGS=""
GOHOSTARCH="amd64"
GOHOSTOS="darwin"
GONOPROXY=""
GONOSUMDB=""
GOOS="darwin"
GOPATH="/Users/scothis/Development/go"
GOPRIVATE=""
GOPROXY="https://proxy.golang.org,direct"
GOROOT="/usr/local/Cellar/go/1.13.4/libexec"
GOSUMDB="sum.golang.org"
GOTMPDIR=""
GOTOOLDIR="/usr/local/Cellar/go/1.13.4/libexec/pkg/tool/darwin_amd64"
GCCGO="gccgo"
AR="ar"
CC="clang"
CXX="clang++"
CGO_ENABLED="1"
GOMOD="<snip>"
CGO_CFLAGS="-g -O2"
CGO_
```

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description:
Test that the fix is working as expected:
```bash
ko version
```
Confirm the feature described in "ko stops building containers after Golang updates" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I think we've improved this situation by surfacing better errors and requiring `ko://`, but I'm not sure if the underlying problem is actually fixed.

GOGCCFLAGS="-fPIC -m64 -pthread -fno-caret-diagnostics -Qunused-arguments -fmessage-length=0 -fdebug-

prerequisites.description: Go 1.21+ and ko installed. ko is a CLI tool for building Go container images, not a Kubernetes workload.
